title: Question: Coverage output syntax options
body:
---


Author Name: **Anton Krug**
Original Redmine Message: 2594 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi,

The documentation about line coverage is bit sparse and maybe assumes everybody knows how to use. I'm bit new to this. I made my own design and have my cpp code "testing/simulating" the logic. It does output the line coverage in the dat file, but I don't know what to do with it, there is verilator_coverage, but it's not clear to me how to use it. If there would be a way to convert these into lcov format I could be able to input these into my editor and show how lines are covered in the IDE. Or maybe generate html reports like lcov does? At the moment I'm only able to read the line coverage with text file and I struggle to find a way how to integrate it into something else or make a human-friendly report, or anything.

I would appreciate any input.

Thank you very much.
